name: Christian Flag

colors.symbolism: the purity and peace of Christ
the canton — faithfulness, truth, and the waters of baptism
the cross — the blood of Christ

description: A white field with a blue canton in the upper hoist bearing a single red Latin cross.
history: Designed in 1897 by Charles Overton, superintendent of a Sunday school in Brooklyn, New York, and popularised by Methodist youth-work secretary Ralph Diffendorfer. It was formally adopted by the (Protestant) Federal Council of Churches in 1942 and is widely used by Protestant denominations across the world; the Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches do not use it.

features.symbolism: the canton with the cross of Christ
